senses.definition: a variation of American football played indoors on a smaller field with eight players per team

examples.x: He plays arena football professionally.
Arena football games are fast-paced and exciting.
She scored a touchdown in her first arena football match.
Arena football fields are smaller than traditional ones.
She joined an arena football team this season.

senses.definition: a non-contact version of American football where players must remove a flag from the ball carrier to end a play instead of tackling

examples.x: She joined a flag football league.
They practiced flag football at the park.
Flag football is a fun, non-contact sport.
She learned the rules of flag football quickly.
Flag football requires skill and strategy.

senses.definition: a pass in American football that happens when a player throws or kicks the ball towards their teammate who is ahead of them on the field

examples.x: The team relies on quick forward passes to move the ball up the field.
She completed a long forward pass to break through the defense.
The soccer midfielder made a forward pass to set up a scoring opportunity.
The quarterback made a perfect forward pass to the wide receiver for a touchdown.
A well-timed forward pass can change the momentum of the game.

senses.definition: (American football) a maneuver where a player runs along the sideline to avoid defenders and advance the ball

examples.x: The running back used an end run to score a touchdown.
The quarterback called for an end run play.
They practiced end runs during training.
She executed an end run to evade tackles.
He tried an end run but stepped out of bounds.

senses.definition: an act of dropping or failing to catch the ball properly

examples.x: His fumble in the final seconds of the game cost his team the championship.
The coach emphasized the importance of minimizing fumbles during practice to improve their chances of winning.

senses.definition: a rushing play where the ball carrier runs toward the area just outside the offensive tackle

examples.x: The defense read the off-tackle run and stopped it for a loss.
He used his speed to break free on the off-tackle run.
The running back executed a powerful off-tackle run for a touchdown.
The running back followed his blockers on the off-tackle run.
The offense called an off-tackle run play to exploit the weak side of the defense.

senses.definition: a practice game in American football

examples.x: They had a scrimmage to try out new plays.
During the scrimmage, the defense played very well.
He hurt his ankle during the scrimmage last week.
The scrimmage ended with a close score.
The scrimmage helped the players feel like they were in a real game.

senses.definition: a play where the quarterback throws the ball to a teammate to advance down the field

examples.x: The pass play resulted in a touchdown.
She completed a pass play for a first down.
The quarterback executed a perfect pass play.
The pass play was well-covered by the defense.

senses.definition: (American football) to obstruct opponents to create space or protect a teammate carrying the ball

examples.x: They used a tight end to run interference on the linebacker.

senses.definition: (American football) the act of passing the ball from the center to the quarterback to start a play

examples.x: The quarterback fumbled the snap and lost the ball.
He called for the snap and quickly threw a pass.
The snap was perfect, allowing the play to start smoothly.
During practice, they worked on timing the snap better.
The center made a high snap, causing a delay in the play.

senses.definition: a successful catch of a pass by a American football player

examples.x: The receiver made a fantastic reception in the end zone.
The crowd cheered loudly after his amazing reception.
His reception helped the team gain a crucial first down.
She secured the reception despite the defender's tight coverage.
The defender intercepted the ball, preventing a reception.

senses.definition: (American football) a successful catch of a pass by a defensive player intended for the opposing team

examples.x: The cornerback's interception changed the course of the game.
His interception prevented a potential scoring drive.
The safety’s interception in the end zone stopped a touchdown.
She celebrated after making a critical interception.
Instant replay confirmed the interception was valid.

senses.definition: (American football) the act of one player handing the ball to another player, typically the quarterback giving it to a running back

examples.x: He sprinted after receiving the handoff from the quarterback.
The running back scored a touchdown after a well-timed handoff.
They practiced handoffs to improve their offensive strategy.
The quarterback executed a smooth handoff to the running back.
The running back was tackled behind the line of scrimmage after the handoff.

senses.definition: the initial chance for the offensive team in American football to advance the ball by at least ten yards from the spot of the last play

examples.x: The team gained a first down on their opening drive.
He caught a pass for a first down.
She ran the ball for a first down.
After achieving a first down, the offense strategically planned their next move.
Catching a pass for a first down, he secured momentum for the offense.

senses.definition: a chance a team has to move the football forward toward the opponent's end zone

examples.x: After three unsuccessful downs, they punted the ball away.

senses.definition: a scoring play where a team attempts to kick the football through the opponent's goalposts

examples.x: The kicker made a 40-yard field goal to tie the game.
They attempted a field goal on fourth down but missed.
The field goal attempt was blocked by the defense.
The team celebrated after a successful field goal in overtime.
They needed a field goal to win the game in the final seconds.

senses.definition: (American football) a scoring play where a player carries or catches the ball into the opponent's end zone, earning six points

examples.x: He sprinted into the end zone for a touchdown.
Her touchdown catch was the highlight of the game.
The team scored multiple touchdowns in the game.
The defense returned an interception for a touchdown.
Their last-minute touchdown secured the win.

senses.definition: an imaginary line where the ball is placed before each play begins

examples.x: They gained yards beyond the line of scrimmage on first down.
The quarterback surveyed the field from the line of scrimmage.
A penalty was called for crossing the line of scrimmage early.
The referee spotted the ball precisely on the line of scrimmage.
Players on both sides of the ball gathered at the line of scrimmage.

senses.definition: the act of scoring extra points after a touchdown in American footbal

examples.x: The kicker missed the conversion, leaving the score unchanged.
He kicked the extra point for the conversion.
The kicker missed the conversion, leaving the score unchanged.
She scored the touchdown and then made the conversion kick.
He faked the handoff and ran in for the two-point conversion.

senses.definition: a play where the quarterback throws the football to a teammate to advance the ball down the field

examples.x: He threw a quick pass to the running back.
The quarterback's accurate pass set up a first down.
The quarterback completed a 20-yard pass to the wide receiver.
His pass floated over the defenders' heads.
The offense relied on quick passes to move the ball efficiently.

senses.definition: (American football) a running play where the ball carrier runs toward the sideline to gain yards

examples.x: The running back executed a sweep to the right.
They used a sweep to gain yards on the outside.
The offense scored a touchdown on a perfectly executed sweep.
He turned the corner on the sweep and sprinted down the sideline.
A well-blocked sweep allowed the team to convert on third down.

senses.definition: (American football) the act of advancing the ball by running with it

examples.x: He fumbled during the carry and the defense recovered the ball.
The quarterback executed a successful carry, gaining 15 yards.
The running back's carry was stopped short by a strong tackle.
His agility during the carry allowed him to evade multiple defenders.
The coach emphasized secure ball handling during every carry drill.

senses.definition: a defensive score worth two points, earned when the offense is tackled in their own end zone

examples.x: The safety gave their team a two-point lead.
His quick tackle resulted in a safety early in the game.
A safety in overtime sealed the victory for the defense.
The offense avoided a safety by punting the ball quickly.
The defense celebrated after the safety was confirmed.

senses.definition: (American football) a loss of yardage for the offense caused by a defensive player tackling the quarterback behind the line of scrimmage

examples.x: The defensive end made a crucial sack in the final minutes of the game.
The fans erupted in cheers as the defense celebrated another sack.
The defensive line pressured the quarterback all game and finally got a sack.
The sack caused a fumble, and the defense recovered the ball.

senses.definition: a defensive tactic in American football where extra defensive players rush toward the quarterback to try to tackle him or disrupt the play before he can pass the ball

examples.x: The defense called a blitz to put pressure on the quarterback.
The safety came on a blitz and sacked the quarterback.
The linebacker timed the blitz perfectly and forced an incomplete pass.
The blitz confused the offensive line, leading to a big loss of yardage.
The coach dialed up a blitz on third down to force a turnover.
